revision.comment: 新しいページ：楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（神奈川県大磯町にある横穴墓群）。
revision.text:
[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa|lat_deg=35|lat_min=19|lat_sec=10.40|lon_deg=139|lon_min=18|lon_sec=51.63|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん、楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）/楊谷寺谷横穴墓群）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下群}}]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

横穴群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん、楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）/楊谷寺谷横穴墓群）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下群}}]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

横穴群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん、楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）/楊谷寺谷横穴墓群）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下群}}]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=220|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん、楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）/楊谷寺谷横穴墓群）は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|下群}}]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん）、または'''楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）・'''楊谷寺谷横穴墓群'''は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|下群}}]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu}} [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|ja|吉見百穴}} (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} (February 2013)]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて..."
revision.text:
{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu}} [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|ja|吉見百穴}} (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28}}</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴墓群}} B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}}: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|ja|楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0}}</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市}}, Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013}}</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|ja|吉見百穴}} in {{ill|Yoshimi|ja|吉見町}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|ja|薄葬令}} before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.comment: making the links work
revision.text:
{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] to [[Category:kkkkk]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>

Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].

By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>

The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|YoCategoryaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio HyaCategorya Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.comment: fixing the kk replacement issue
revision.text:
{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. They were carved into rock faces, featuring entrances leading to passageways (羨道, sendō), which open into burial chambers (玄室, genshitsu). These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].
== Origins and Evolution == Yokohama tombs originated in northern Kyushu around the late 5th century during the Kofun period and subsequently spread across western Japan, including the San'in, Sanyo, Kinki, and Tokai regions by the mid-6th century. By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>
== Name == Yokohama tombs are sometimes called "yokoana kofun" (横穴古墳), but technically the term "kofun" refers to burial mounds, which these tombs lack. They are often grouped under broader definitions of kofun but differ significantly in their construction and purpose.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

{{distinguish|Yokokutsu-shiki sekishitsu]] [[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[File:Yoshimi Yoshimi-hyakuana 2.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] (November 2010)]] [[File:柏谷横穴群.JPG|right|250px|thumb|{{ill|Kashiya Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] (February 2013)]]
'''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. == Characteristics == [[File:市ヶ尾横穴墓群B群.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Ichigao Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴墓群]] B Cluster: Forecourt and entrance.]] [[File:出山横穴墓群 2009.03.21 - panoramio (5).jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Izuyama Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]]: Interior view of the burial chamber.]] [[File:長柄横穴群11号墓棺座.JPG|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Nagara Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] Tomb 11: Stone coffin base.]] Yokohama tombs closely resemble [[kofun]] tombs with horizontal stone chambers. These tombs typically lack burial mounds but include exceptions. Burial chambers often feature bases for coffins (棺座, kanza) and showcase various ceiling styles, such as house-shaped, dome-shaped, or arch-shaped. Some tombs incorporate forecourts and intermediate rooms between the passageway and chamber.<ref name="#1">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Keiji Nagahara|editor=Eiichi Ishigami|title=Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|year=1999|isbn=4-00-080093-0]]</ref>
In {{ill|Kamakura|en}}[[ja:鎌倉市]], Yokohama tombs feature the distinctive "Kamakura-type" (鎌倉型横穴墓), where burial shelves or smaller inner chambers were carved into the rear walls of burial chambers.<ref name="#2">{{Cite book|language=ja|author=Sakae Ward Historical Committee|title=The History of Sakae|publisher=Sakae Ward Community Promotion Division|year=2013]]</ref>
Yokohama tombs are typically found in clusters (横穴墓群, yokohama bokun). The {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] in {{ill|Yoshimi|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en}}[[ja:埼玉県]], comprises over 200 tombs and is a prominent example designated as a National Historic Site.
Some tombs also feature wall carvings and paintings, often categorized as decorative kofun. Colored embellishments have been discovered in regions such as [[Kyushu]], [[Kanto]], and parts of [[Tohoku]].

By the early 7th century, they had reached the Hokuriku, Kanto, and southern Tohoku regions. The practice peaked around the time of the {{ill|Thin Burial Edict|en}}[[ja:薄葬令]] before declining in the late 8th century.<ref name="#1"/>

The term "yokohama tombs" became common in the 1960s, gaining popularity among researchers in eastern Japan, whereas western Japan researchers traditionally referred to them as "yokoana." The term "yokohama" is preferred for consistency.<ref name="rekihaku"/>
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) -
