revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)}}

[[Category:Rock-cut tombs]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ōiso Town]] [[Category:Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]]

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:黒岩横穴墓群]] (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十郎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en}}[[ja:鳩山町]], Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝之城横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en}}[[ja:所沢市]], Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北秋津横穴墓群]] (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:比丘尼山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en}}[[ja:東松山市]], Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群||en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]]

'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群||en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]

'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en}}[[ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群]] Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]] [[ja:吉見百穴]] [[ja:柏谷横穴群]] '''Yokohama Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Yokouana Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:山畑横穴群]] ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en}}[[ja:大崎市]], [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:愛宕山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en}}[[ja:太白区]], {{ill|Sendai|en}}[[ja:仙台市]]) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:茂ヶ崎横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宗禅寺横穴墓群]] (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:清戸迫横穴墓]] ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:双葉町]], [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:泉崎横穴墓]] ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en}}[[ja:西白河郡]], {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:泉崎村]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:中田横穴]] ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en}}[[ja:いわき市]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:羽山横穴]] ({{ill|Minamisōma|en}}[[ja:南相馬市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:和田大仏及び横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sukagawa|en}}[[ja:須賀川市]], Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十五郎穴横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en}}[[ja:ひたちなか市]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:唐御所横穴]] ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en}}[[ja:那珂川町]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|en}}[[ja:長岡百穴古墳]] ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en}}[[ja:宇都宮市]], Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|en}}[[ja:吉見百穴]] ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en}}[[ja:吉見町]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]) -
