revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[rock-cut tombs]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
'''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgorō-ana Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karanogosho Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
