revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

revision.comment: 動画
revision.text:
[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん）、または'''楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）・'''楊谷寺谷横穴墓群'''は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|下群}}]] [[ファイル:Yokokuji Yato Caves - Kanagawa - 2023 April 27.webm|thumb|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=三瓶裕司・柏木善治|title=楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群の家形横穴墓の構造 -三次元計測とそれに基づく遡源形態の試論-|date=2022|journal=情報考古学|publisher=日本情報考古学会|volume=27|number=1・2|pages=22-35|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Remove {{translated page}} (ja redirect) + cat
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.

== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.

{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[Category:qq]][[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 up to rev 105572247
revision.text:
[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん）、または'''楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）・'''楊谷寺谷横穴墓群'''は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|下群}}]] [[ファイル:Yokokuji Yato Caves - Kanagawa - 2023 April 27.webm|thumb|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=三瓶裕司・柏木善治|title=楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群の家形横穴墓の構造 -三次元計測とそれに基づく遡源形態の試論-|date=2022|journal=情報考古学|publisher=日本情報考古学会|volume=27|number=1・2|pages=22-35|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[楊谷寺谷戸横穴群]] to [[Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 概観}}{{small|手前に下群（下2段）、右上に上群（上2段）。}}]] {{Location map|Japan Kanagawa Prefecture|label=楊谷寺谷戸
横穴群|position=top|width=250|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|relief=1|caption={{Center|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の位置}}}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E}}}} '''楊谷寺谷戸横穴群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぐん）、または'''楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群'''（ようこくじやとおうけつぼぐん）・'''楊谷寺谷横穴墓群'''は、[[神奈川県]][[中郡]][[大磯町]]大磯にある[[横穴墓]]群。神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|上群}}]] [[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group, gaikan-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|下群}}]] [[ファイル:Yokokuji Yato Caves - Kanagawa - 2023 April 27.webm|thumb|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群]] 神奈川県南部、全国屈指の横穴墓の密集地域として知られる[[大磯丘陵]]の東部、高麗山支谷の丘陵南腹に営造された横穴墓群である。4段にわたり27基が確認されている（7基は土の流入で埋没）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。[[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）・[[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）に調査が実施されている。 本横穴墓群は、平面方形で切妻造家形天井の11号に始まり、次いで四方の長押の上に寄棟造が退化したドーム形天井の5号、さらに退化したアーチ形断面の筒形天井の4・7号などの順の構築と推測される史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会、1982年設置）。。いずれも盗掘に遭っているため、断片的な副葬品以外は詳らかでない{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}。研究史上著名かつ大磯丘陵では代表的な横穴墓群であるとともに、様々な内部構造の横穴墓から構成されることから、形態の変遷過程の基準としても重要視される遺跡になる{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}史跡説明板（大磯町教育委員会、2010年設置）。。 遺跡域は[[1966年]]（昭和41年）に神奈川県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）、[[坪井正五郎]]による調査{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1962年]]（[[昭和]]37年）、調査（[[赤星直忠]]・大磯町教育委員会、[[1964年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）7月19日、神奈川県指定史跡に指定。 == 主な横穴墓 == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! !開口部 !内部 |- |'''11号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''5号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''4号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''7号''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |} == 文化財 == === 神奈川県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群 - 1966年（昭和41年）7月19日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf 指定（選択・登録）文化財一覧）]}}（大磯町ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * 大磯町郷土博物館（大磯町西小磯、県立大磯城山公園内） - 楊谷寺谷戸横穴群の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（神奈川県教育委員会1982年設置板、大磯町教育委員会2010年設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 14 神奈川県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷戸横穴群（平凡社）|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大谷猛|year=1989|chapter=楊谷寺谷横穴墓群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|楊谷寺谷横穴墓群（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1964|chapter=|title=神奈川県大磯町の横穴（大磯町文化財調査報告 第1冊）|publisher=大磯町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=三瓶裕司・柏木善治|title=楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群の家形横穴墓の構造 -三次元計測とそれに基づく遡源形態の試論-|date=2022|journal=情報考古学|publisher=日本情報考古学会|volume=27|number=1・2|pages=22-35|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * [[釜口古墳 (大磯町)]] {{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=楊谷寺谷戸横穴群}} {{デフォルトソート:ようこくしやとおうけつくん}} [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:神奈川県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Daibutsu and Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukagawa|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgorō-ana Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|qq=}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karanogosho Cave Tomb|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karanogosho Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|qq=}}, [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Cave Tomb Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hundred Caves|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qq=}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimi, Saitama|qq=}}, [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroiwa Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrō Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatoyama, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takinojō Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokorozawa|qq=}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaakitsu Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bikuniyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashimatsuyama|qq=}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya|qq=}}, Saitama) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tama, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[en:Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster]][[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴群), also known as '''Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group''' (楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群) or '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Burial Group''', is a group of [[:en:Corridor-type kofun|Corridor-type kofun]] located in [[Ōiso, Naka District, Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site.[[File:楊谷寺谷戸横穴墓群11号墓.jpg|250px|right|thumb|{{ill|Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Cave Tomb Cluster|lt=Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōkokuji Valley Yokana Cluster|qq=}} Tomb 11: House-shaped chamber.]]
'''Cave Tombs''' (横穴墓, Yokōbo/Ouketsubo<ref name="rekihaku">{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/maibun/qa/detail.php?seq=22|title=Q：横穴墓ってなんて読むのですか?|publisher=Yokohama City Foundation for History and Culture|accessdate=2018-07-28]]</ref>) are burial tombs generally carved into slopes or cliffs, typically measuring around 2 meters in height and extending several meters in depth. These tombs served as burial chambers in ancient East Asia and are most commonly associated with the [[Kofun period]] in Japan, where this burial type emerged and became widely used.
== Overview == The tomb group is situated on the southern slope of the ridges in the [[Ōiso Hills]], an area known for having one of Japan’s highest concentrations of rock-cut tombs. Located in a tributary valley of [[Koma Mountain]], 27 tombs have been identified, spread across four tiers (seven of which have been buried by soil deposition){{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa"/>. Investigations were conducted in [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12) and [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37).
This burial group features a variety of architectural styles, beginning with tomb 11, which has a square layout with a gabled-roof design. This evolved into tomb 5, which has a dome-shaped ceiling—a simplified version of the hip-and-gable roof. Further simplifications resulted in the arch-shaped ceilings of tombs 4 and 7<ref name="Explanation Board Kanagawa">Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982).</ref>. Due to looting, only fragmented grave goods remain{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}. As one of the most well-known and representative burial groups in the Ōiso Hills, it is considered significant for studying the structural evolution of rock-cut tombs{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}<ref name="Explanation Board Ōiso Town">Historic Site Explanation Board (Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010).</ref>.
In [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), the site was designated a Historic Site by Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Excavation History == * [[:en:1879|1879]] (Meiji 12): Investigated by [[Tsuboi Shōgorō]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1962|1962]] (Shōwa 37): Survey conducted by [[Akaboshi Naotada]] and the Ōiso Town Board of Education, with a report published in [[:en:1964|1964]]{{Sfn|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}. * [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41), July 19: Designated as a Kanagawa Prefecture Historic Site<ref name="Cultural Property List"/>.
== Major Rock-Cut Tombs == {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |- ! Tomb !! Entrance !! Interior |- |'''Tomb 11''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-11-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 5''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-5-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 4''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-4-2.jpg|200px]] |- |'''Tomb 7''' |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-1.jpg|200px]] |[[File:Yokokuji-yato Yokoana Group-7-2.jpg|200px]] |- |}
== Cultural Property == === Kanagawa Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** '''Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' - Designated on July 19, [[:en:1966|1966]] (Shōwa 41)<ref name="Cultural Property List">{{PDFlink|[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/material/files/group/30/bunkazai21218.pdf Designated Cultural Properties List]}} (Ōiso Town Website).</ref>.
== Related Facilities == * '''Ōiso Town Local History Museum''' (Nishi-Oiso, Ōiso Town, within the Kanagawa Prefectural Ōiso Shiroyama Park) - Stores and displays artifacts from Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for this article)}} * Historic Site Explanation Board (Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education, 1982; Ōiso Town Board of Education, 2010) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1984|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 14 Kanagawa no Chimei|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582910335|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group (Heibonsha)|1984}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Ōtani Takeshi|year=1989|chapter=Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group|title=[[Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Yōkokuji-Yato Rock-Cut Tomb Group (Kofun)|1989}}}}
== Related Literature == {{small|(Literature not directly used for this article)}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1964|title=Rock-Cut Tombs of Ōiso, Kanagawa Prefecture (Ōiso Town Cultural Property Survey Report Vol. 1)|publisher=Ōiso Town Board of Education|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Sanpei Yūji, Kashiwagi Zenji|title=Structure of House-Shaped Rock-Cut Tombs in Yōkokuji-Yato: A Study on Three-Dimensional Measurement and Morphological Analysis|date=2022|journal=Jōhō Kōkogaku|publisher=Nihon Jōhō Kōkogaku-kai|volume=27|number=1-2|pages=22-35|ref=}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Yōkokuji-Yato ōketsu-gun}} * {{ill|Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|ja|釜口古墳 (大磯町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiguchi Kofun (Ōiso Town)|qq=|1=Kamaguchi Kofun (Oiso)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108454181}}
{{Coord|35|19|10.40|N|139|18|51.63|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=title|name=Yōkokuji-Yato Cave Tomb Group}} == Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahata Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki, Miyagi|qq=}}, [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Cave Tombs|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atagoyama Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihaku-ku, Sendai|qq=}}, {{ill|Sendai|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai|qq=}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mogasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzenji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotosaku Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futaba, Fukushima|qq=}}, [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Cave Tomb|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki Cave Tomb|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumizaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakata Cave Tombs|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakata Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}) -
